questionText:
Transform the following sentence into reported speech:
"I am tired," she said.
options: She said that she is tired.
She said that she was tired.
She said that she were tired.
She said that she has been tired.
explanation: When transforming direct speech into reported speech, we apply the tense backshift rule. Present Simple becomes Past Simple. 'I am' becomes 'she was'. Option 1 is incorrect because it doesn't apply the backshift. Option 3 uses 'were' incorrectly. Option 4 changes the tense to Present Perfect, which is not the correct backshift.

questionText:
Choose the correct reported speech for:
"We have finished our homework," they said.
options: They said that they have finished their homework.
They said that they had finished their homework.
They said that they finished their homework.
They said that they were finishing their homework.
explanation: Present Perfect (have finished) becomes Past Perfect (had finished) in reported speech. Option 1 keeps the Present Perfect (incorrect). Option 3 uses Past Simple (incorrect for present perfect). Option 4 uses Past Continuous (wrong tense).

questionText:
Transform into reported speech:
"I will call you tomorrow," he told me.
options: He told me that he will call me tomorrow.
He told me that he would call me tomorrow.
He told me that he would call me the next day.
He told me that he will call me the next day.
explanation: In reported speech, Future Simple (will) becomes would. Additionally, 'tomorrow' changes to 'the next day' (or 'the following day'). Option 2 changes 'will' to 'would' but keeps 'tomorrow' (incorrect for time shift). Option 3 correctly changes both the tense and the time expression. Option 4 is incorrect because 'will' is not backshifted.

questionText:
Which of the following is the correct reported speech for:
"I can speak French," she said.
options: She said that she can speak French.
She said that she could speak French.
She said that she would speak French.
She said that she has spoken French.
explanation: Modal verbs also change in reported speech. 'Can' becomes 'could' (backshift). Option 1 keeps 'can' (incorrect). Option 3 changes to 'would' (not the correct modal). Option 4 changes to Present Perfect (completely different tense).

questionText:
Report the following statement correctly:
"I went to the market yesterday," she said.
options: She said that she went to the market yesterday.
She said that she had gone to the market the day before.
She said that she has gone to the market yesterday.
She said that she had gone to the market yesterday.
explanation: Past Simple becomes Past Perfect in reported speech. 'Yesterday' changes to 'the day before'. Option 1 keeps 'went' and 'yesterday' (incorrect). Option 2 correctly uses 'had gone' and 'the day before'. Option 3 uses Present Perfect (incorrect). Option 4 uses Past Perfect but keeps 'yesterday' (incorrect time shift).

questionText:
Transform into reported speech:
"I don't like this food," he said.
options: He said that he doesn't like this food.
He said that he didn't like this food.
He said that he didn't like that food.
He said that he doesn't like that food.
explanation: Present Simple becomes Past Simple (don't like → didn't like). The demonstrative 'this' changes to 'that' in reported speech. Option 3 correctly changes both the tense and the demonstrative. Option 2 changes the tense but keeps 'this' (incorrect). Option 1 doesn't backshift. Option 4 has a tense error.

questionText:
Which pronoun change is correct in reported speech?
"I love you," she said to him.
options: She said to him that I love him.
She said to him that she loved him.
She said to him that she loves you.
She said to him that I loved you.
explanation: Pronouns must be adapted in reported speech. 'I' changes to 'she' (referring to the speaker). 'You' changes to 'him' (the person being spoken to). The verb 'love' changes to 'loved' (backshift). Option 1 incorrectly uses 'I love him'. Option 3 uses 'loves you' (incorrect pronouns and tense). Option 4 is completely incorrect with 'I loved you'.

questionText:
Identify the correct reported speech with proper tense backshift:
"I am going to visit my grandmother," she said.
options: She said that she is going to visit my grandmother.
She said that she was going to visit her grandmother.
She said that she will go to visit her grandmother.
She said that she would go to visit her grandmother.
explanation: Present Continuous (am going) becomes Past Continuous (was going) in reported speech. The possessive 'my' changes to 'her' to match the subject. Option 1 keeps Present Continuous and uses 'my' (incorrect). Option 3 changes to Future. Option 4 uses 'would go' (not the correct backshift for 'going to').

questionText:
Which sentence correctly reports a statement in the Past Perfect?
"I had already eaten," she said.
options: She said that she had already eaten.
She said that she has already eaten.
She said that she already ate.
She said that she had already ate.
explanation: Past Perfect remains Past Perfect in reported speech (no further backshift is necessary). Option 0 correctly keeps the Past Perfect. Option 1 changes to Present Perfect (incorrect). Option 2 changes to Past Simple (incorrect). Option 3 uses 'had already ate' (incorrect past participle - should be 'eaten').

questionText:
Transform the following into reported speech with correct time expression change:
"I will be here at 3 PM today," he said.
options: He said that he will be here at 3 PM today.
He said that he would be here at 3 PM that day.
He said that he would be there at 3 PM that day.
He said that he would be there at 3 PM today.
explanation: In reported speech, 'will' becomes 'would', 'here' becomes 'there', and 'today' becomes 'that day'. Option 3 correctly applies all transformations. Option 2 changes 'will' to 'would' and 'today' to 'that day' but keeps 'here' (incorrect). Option 1 doesn't apply any backshift. Option 4 keeps 'today' (incorrect time shift).

questionText:
Choose the correct reported speech for:
"I bought this car two years ago," he said.
options: He said that he bought that car two years before.
He said that he had bought that car two years before.
He said that he had bought this car two years ago.
He said that he bought this car two years before.
explanation: Past Simple becomes Past Perfect (bought → had bought). 'This' becomes 'that' and 'ago' becomes 'before'. Option 1 uses Past Simple (incorrect). Option 2 correctly applies all transformations. Option 3 keeps 'this' and 'ago' (incorrect). Option 4 keeps 'this' and uses Past Simple (incorrect).

questionText:
Report the following statement correctly:
"We are playing football now," they said.
options: They said that they are playing football now.
They said that they were playing football then.
They said that they played football then.
They said that they have played football now.
explanation: Present Continuous (are playing) becomes Past Continuous (were playing) in reported speech. 'Now' changes to 'then'. Option 1 keeps Present Continuous and 'now' (incorrect). Option 2 correctly applies both transformations. Option 3 changes to Past Simple (incorrect tense). Option 4 changes to Present Perfect (incorrect tense).
